findings.description: Duplicated separator-insertion logic in slugify loop
findings.evidence: The block `if prev_sep && !out.is_empty() { out.push('-'); }` is repeated verbatim in the lowercase ASCII arm, the uppercase ASCII arm, and again after the match for transliteration arms. This asymmetry (ASCII arms use `continue`, transliteration arms fall through) makes the control flow harder to follow and means any change to separator logic must be applied in three places.
findings.recommendation: Restructure the loop so token production is separated from separator insertion, or extract a small closure/helper for the hyphen insertion to eliminate the duplication and make the flow uniform.
